text.title: Compliance Matrix — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules governing matrix construction, freshness, and the legal-review boundary.

rule.statement: The matrix has EXACTLY 7 rows in fixed order: (1) ENTITY-REGISTRATION, (2) DATA-PROCESSING-BASIS, (3) DPA / PROCESSOR-TERMS, (4) COOKIE / CONSENT, (5) MARKETING-EMAIL, (6) AI-ACT / AUTOMATED-DECISIONS, (7) SALES-TAX / VAT. Skipping a row leaves the founder blind to a real obligation.

rationale.source: GDPR Art. 5/6/13/30 + CCPA 1798.100 + EU AI Act 2024 + IRS / HMRC tax rules

rule.rationale: These 7 rows cover ~90% of routine micro-agency obligations across the three jurisdictions. Reducing to fewer rows leaves common gaps (cookie banner, AI act); adding more rows pushes into specialist sector regs.

rule.statement: EVERY row of EVERY emitted matrix MUST be marked `requires_legal_review: true` and prefixed with the disclaimer "NOT LEGAL ADVICE — this is a structured starting point for counsel review". Outputs MUST NOT be presented as the final compliance position.

rationale.source: ABA Model Rule 5.5 / SRA Code of Conduct / EU lawyer regulations

rule.rationale: An LLM emitting compliance positions without disclaimer creates legal-exposure for the operator AND potentially for the methodology distributor. The disclaimer is a hard requirement, not optional copy.

rule.statement: Per row, the matrix MUST evaluate threshold applicability with current numeric thresholds: CCPA — annual gross revenue > $25M OR 100k+ consumers OR 50%+ revenue from selling PII; UK-GDPR — any processing of UK residents' data; EU GDPR — any processing of EU residents' data; EU AI Act — high-risk system OR GPAI provider classification.

rationale.source: CCPA §1798.140 / GDPR Art. 3 / EU AI Act Art. 6 + 51

rule.rationale: Generic "you might need this" reduces signal; threshold-conditional output tells the founder WHICH rows actually bind them today.

rule.statement: Source data (statute references, thresholds, fees) MUST cite an effective date no older than 18 months. Statutes change (CPRA 2023, EU AI Act 2024, US state privacy expansions). Stale data → wrong recommendations.
rule.rationale: US state privacy laws shipped 13+ statutes between 2020-2026 (CA, VA, CO, CT, UT, OR, TX, MT, IA, IN, TN, FL, DE). 18-month staleness window catches the major shifts.

rule.statement: Every binding row (where threshold check passes) MUST emit a SPECIFIC first action with an owner role and an artifact, not generic "consult counsel". Examples: "Update DPA template with EU Standard Contractual Clauses 2021/914 (owner: legal/ops; artifact: DPA-v3.docx)".
rule.rationale: Compliance gets shelved when actions are abstract. Concrete first-action moves the matrix from "good chart" to "executed change".

example.title: Row-3 (DPA / processor terms) example
example.code:
Row: DPA / processor terms
| Jurisdiction | Obligation | Threshold | |--------------|-------------------------------------------|---------------| | US | None federally; CCPA §1798.140 if "service provider" | CCPA-bound only | | UK | UK-GDPR Art. 28 — written contract | Any UK data | | EU | GDPR Art. 28 — written DPA + SCCs for transfers | Any EU data |
First action (if bound): Update DPA template: - Art. 28(3) processor obligations clauses - EU SCCs 2021/914 modules 2 (controller→processor) and 4 (processor→subprocessor) as applicable - UK IDTA / Addendum for UK transfers Owner: legal/ops Artifact: DPA-v3.docx requires_legal_review: true
example.note: r1-r5 all visible: row coverage, threshold-tested, concrete first action, legal-review flag.

example.title: Generic
example.code:
Row: DPA terms
"You should consider whether you need a Data Processing Agreement with your vendors. Consult legal counsel."
(No threshold check, no specific action, no artifact, no jurisdiction breakdown.)
example.note: Useless. Generic compliance text doesn't get acted on; the matrix shelf-rots.
